zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret de Clare於大約1208年出生在英國肯特通 卡素時，她的父親Sir Thomas De Clare20歲，母親Juliana FitzMaurice25歲。她於大約1227年與Bartholomew de Badlesmere在英國肯特結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於大約1258年在英國肯特享年52歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭肯特迪亞爾聖 倫納德 亞德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret de Clare于大约1208年出生在英国肯特通 卡素时，她的父亲Sir Thomas De Clare20岁，母亲Juliana FitzMaurice25岁。她于大约1227年与Bartholomew de Badlesmere在英国肯特结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于大约1258年在英国肯特享年52岁，埋葬在英国英格兰肯特迪亞爾聖 倫納德 亞德。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Clare
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Clare, Wicklow, and Wexford): habitational name from Clare in Suffolk (an eastern county of England), the center of a major Norman baronial estate since the Norman Conquest. The English placename probably originated as a Celtic river name meaning ‘bright, gentle, or warm’. The surname is not from the Irish county name Clare, which is either from the surname or from a place called with Gaelic clár ‘plank bridge’. Another Norman surname in the southeast of Ireland was de Clere, from Clères in Seine-Maritime (France), named from the local river.

English and French: from the Middle English, Old French female personal name Cla(i)re (from Latin Clara, from clarus ‘clear, bright, famous’) which achieved some popularity, greater elsewhere in Europe than in England, through the fame of Saint Clare of Assisi (see Chiara ). The English surname seems to have been interchangeable with Clear 1.

English: occupational name from an agent derivative of Middle English cley ‘clay’. The work of medieval clayers involved plastering a framework of interwoven twigs with mud to produce wattle-and-daub work.

variants.name: Claire
meanings: Irish: variant of   Clare  .
Variant of   Clair  , an altered form of German   Klare  .
French: variant of   Clair  .

variants.name: Chiara
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Marco, Amedeo, Angelo, Gino, Rocco.

Italian: from the female personal name Chiara or Clara, a name popularized in the medieval period by the cult of Saint Clare of Assisi (c. 1193–1253); she was an associate of Saint Francis of Assisi and founded the order of nuns known as the Poor Clares. See also Chiaro .

variants.name: Claar
meanings: German: variant of Klaar (and, in North America, an altered form of this), a nickname from Middle High German, Middle Low German klar ‘pure, pretty, sensible’, or a habitational name from a minor place so named near Braunschweig.
Possibly also an Americanized form of Dutch Klaar: from a vernacular form of the female personal name Clara (see Clare 2).

variants.name: Clarkin
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): from a pet form of the personal name Clare (compare Clare and Clear ), which is both a masculine and a female personal name in Middle English.
Irish (Monaghan): variant of   Clerkin  .

variants.name: Clear
meanings: English (southeastern England, of Norman origin): from Clere, the French popular form of the personal name Clare 2, and possibly interchangeable with it or confused with it.

English (southeastern): probably a habitational name from clere, a component of several placenames in north Hampshire (Highclere, Burghclere, Kingsclere). This is probably from a Celtic stream name meaning ‘bright’ (cognate with Latin clarus ‘clear, bright’).
Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Klar   1.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret de Clare was born about 1208, in Tong Castle, Kent, England, her father, Sir Thomas De Clare, was 20 and her mother, Juliana FitzMaurice, was 25. She married Bartholomew de Badlesmere about 1227, in Kent, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. She died about 1258, in Kent, England, at the age of 52, and was buried in Saint Leonard Churchyard, Deal, Kent, England, United Kingdom.

Elle est décédée environ 1258, à Kent, Angleterre, à l’âge de 52 ans, et a été enterrée à Saint Leonard Churchyard, Deal, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret de Clareが約1208年にイギリスケントトング カストルで生まれた時，父Sir Thomas De Clareは20歳，母Juliana FitzMauriceは25歳であった。約1227年にイギリスケントでBartholomew de Badlesmereと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は約1258年にイギリスケントで52歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスケントディールサイント レオナルド チャーチヤルドにて埋葬された。

ru.St. Leonard Churchyard, Badlesmare, Kent: сеинт леонард чурчярд, диль, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret de Clare родилась около 1208 г. в местности тунг кастл, кэнт, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Thomas De Clare было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Juliana FitzMaurice, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Bartholomew de Badlesmere около 1227 г. в местности кэнт, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла около 1258 г. в местности кэнт, Англия в возрасте 52 лет и была похоронена в местности сеинт леонард чурчярд, диль, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания.
